item.title: The war of unintended consequences
item.description: Israel and the US have achieved many military aims in Iran, but the unpredictable fallout poses threats to everyone

item.title: We’ll always have Dubai
item.description: Why the newly endangered city will endure

item.title: Allowing 401ks to invest in private markets is a bad move at a bad time
item.description: The move might help asset managers but hurt savers and the economy more broadly

item.title: How mackerel became Schrödinger’s fish
item.description: We sure as hell aren’t buying that much in Waitrose

item.title: Amid European energy fears, coal creeps back into favour
item.description: A protracted conflict in the Middle East would dent expectations that global coal consumption will start to fall

item.title: Britain is now the home of the Middle Man
item.description: Rules don’t even have to be effective to provide gainful employment for advisers

item.title: America rethinks how to train its workforce
item.description: A ‘skills gap’ is not some blight on free enterprise that someone else will fix

item.title: Are attention spans really shrinking?
item.description: Bear with me for 47 seconds — here’s why our fizzing brains still have the ability to focus

item.title: Why we should embrace human complexity
item.description: Sometimes we forget to acknowledge our multiple identities

item.title: Notebook, Abu Dhabi: Where the UAE’s working classes eat — restaurant review
item.description: Forget the flash options. This Keralan chain restaurant in a mall is your key to the city

item.title: It’s time for wine lovers to get over ‘Zinfandel cringe’
item.description: You’d be hard-pushed to find better-value old vine wine

item.title: A turbulent week for financial markets
item.description: Investors risk being too complacent about war in the Middle East

item.title: Is the nightmare scenario for global energy here?
item.description: The Iran crisis is unfolding in a world in which oil and gas supplies are more diversified than in previous decades

item.title: Investors are not ready for a true shock
item.description: The consensus view of the impact of the Iran war on equities and bonds may well prove too sanguine

item.title: Robinhood brings private markets’ valuation problem to the masses

item.description: The Gorton and Denton by-election confirmed just how capricious our electoral system has become

item.description: Don’t panic about gyrating markets, work through this personal finance checklist instead

item.description: Think like a family office and take an intergenerational view

item.description: Many have tried but few succeed

item.description: Plus Korea’s rollercoaster market

item.description: Chaos and instability are a feature, not a bug, of the US president’s approach to foreign policy

item.title: The Iran war presents a ‘guns and butter’ nightmare for investors
item.description: Conflict will intensify the mounting global debt and inflation risks

item.title: Why oil at $200 a barrel is no longer unthinkable
item.description: If supply disruptions persist, holders of stockpiled reserves can’t be relied on to fully cushion the impact

item.title: Will your garden endure as your legacy?
item.description: As life replaces death in springtime, gardeners can’t help but ponder some existential questions

item.title: The AI PR problem
item.description: Sector companies have been slow to address the concerns of ordinary people over the technology
